topics: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing and valuation
Elon Web Services / AI compute business
Nvidia Q1 earnings and market position
AI-driven workforce displacement and CEO messaging

claims.claim: SpaceX is aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation, which would make it the largest IPO ever by more than double Saudi Aramco's $29 billion IPO.
evidence: Largest IPO ever by more than double Aramco's $29B IPO
Expected mid-June, likely June 12, ticker SPCX
Polymarket gives 71% chance SpaceX closes first day above $2 trillion market cap

claims.claim: Anthropic is paying SpaceX $1.25 billion per month to rent Colossus One and parts of Colossus Two, totaling a $45 billion deal over three years ($15 billion/year).
evidence: $45 billion deal over three years
$15 billion per year — effectively adding a second Starlink in revenue
Contract can be canceled by either party with 90 days notice

claims.claim: SpaceX builds data centers dramatically faster than competitors and at lower cost, with construction times dropping from 122 days to 91 days to 66 days across successive facilities.
evidence: First data center: 122 days
Second: 91 days
Third: 66 days

claims.claim: Cursor's Composer 2.5 model became Pareto dominant on AI coding benchmarks after only 3-4 weeks of reinforcement learning on Colossus Two using Cursor's proprietary coding data.
evidence: Composer 2.5 is the same base model as Composer 2 (Kami k.25) with only RL applied
Cursor allegedly has more tokens of coding data than exists on the public internet
Composer 2.5 is the most selected model on Cursor

claims.claim: Nvidia reported Q1 revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year and 20% quarter-over-quarter, with $58 billion net income, $48 billion free cash flow, and 75% gross margins.
evidence: $81.6B revenue, 85% YoY growth
$58B net income, $48B free cash flow
75% gross margins, $5.3 trillion market cap

claims.claim: Nvidia's CPU business is expected to hit $20 billion this year, making it overnight one of the world's largest CPU manufacturers.
evidence: Nvidia announced $20B CPU business projection for the year
Attributed to Nvidia's unique position working with every AI lab enabling co-design

claims.claim: The AI compute market is cross-sectionally inefficient, as valuations of power/cooling/optical companies and DRAM/Nvidia cannot simultaneously be correct.
evidence: Memory makers at 3-5x PE, Nvidia at low PE
Power, cooling, optical names discounting very different futures than chip companies
If power/cooling multiples are correct, Nvidia and memory go up a lot; if Nvidia multiples are correct, everything else underperforms

claims.claim: Starlink generated $11.4 billion in revenue in 2024 on 50% growth with $4.4 billion in operating income and over 10 million subscribers.
evidence: $11.4B revenue, 50% growth
$4.4B operating income
10 million paying subscribers

quote_candidates.text: the most you can hope for here is you keep this job for some amount of time and train your way out of it
You reduce humans to a label called the measurer. And then you're like, I'm going to lay off all the measurers.
We're listening too much to the inventors of AI. I know that's appealing. They're geniuses. They're smart. We need to be listening to the frontline factory workers who are using AI
quote_candidates.speaker: Sham Sankar

quote_candidates.text: Anthropic is paying SpaceX wait for it 1.25 billion a month to rent out Colossus one and parts of Colossus two. It's a $45 billion deal over three years 15 billion a year.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jason Calacanis

quote_candidates.text: they build data centers dramatically faster than anyone else at a lower cost
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

quote_candidates.text: composer 2.5 is literally well outside the Pareto frontier. And that's after three weeks.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker
